title: Deliciously Crisp Peruvian Lamb Seco De Cordero
description: A macrobiotic friendly Peruvian dish featuring lamb, plum jam, and dashi extract, this seco de cordero is a crowd pleaser with its fermented and crisp texture. It is a unique and savory meal option.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together soy sauce, dashi extract, plum jam, garlic, ginger, brown sugar, and black pepper to make the marinade.
Add the lamb pieces to the marinade and mix well to coat, then cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Remove the lamb from the marinade and pat dry with paper towels.
In a large skillet, heat the vegetable oil over medium high heat and sear the lamb pieces until browned on all sides.
Transfer the lamb to a baking dish and roast in the oven for 20 minutes.
While the lamb is roasting, mix the cornstarch with a small amount of water to make a slurry.
After the lamb has roasted for 20 minutes, brush the cornstarch slurry evenly over the lamb pieces.
Return the lamb to the oven and continue roasting for an additional 10 minutes, or until the lamb is crispy and golden brown.
Remove the lamb from the oven and let rest for 5 minutes before serving.
